_comment: Claude Code settings.json — reference wiring for SIEMPLE-AI hooks.
Or copy to: ~/.claude/settings.json for global activation.

PostToolUse._comment: Trigger security scan after any file write or edit.

hooks._note: Claude Code passes the written file path via the CLAUDE_FILE_PATH env var. The hook reads this to determine what to scan.

PreToolUse._comment: Verbose permission fingerprint before any Bash execution.

hooks._note: Hook reads the proposed command from stdin JSON (.tool_input.command) and emits a structured behavioral fingerprint before execution. See claude-code/rules/verbose-permissions.md.

env._note: Set HOOK_STRICT=true to block on MEDIUM semgrep findings (default blocks HIGH+CRITICAL only).

PostToolUse_flow: 3. PostToolUse hook fires with file path in environment
4. post-tool-use.sh checks SCANNABLE_EXTENSIONS, skips non-code files
5. semgrep runs against the written file using claude-code/hooks/semgrep.yaml
6. If HIGH or CRITICAL findings: hook exits non-zero, Claude Code surfaces violation
7. Model must remediate before output is accepted
PreToolUse_flow: 1. Claude Code is about to call Bash tool
2. PreToolUse hook fires with proposed command in stdin JSON
3. verbose-permissions-check.sh decomposes command into behavioral fingerprint
4. Fingerprint shown to user (Service, Program, Directory, Network, Privileges, Signatures, Risk Level)
5. User approves or denies before command executes
